commitMessage:
Remove Java 1.2 and 1.4 checks from JavaModelUtil

Since ECJ min supported version is 1.8 all other code mandates 1.8+ too.

Part of https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/1685
categoryReason: Refactoring of internal JDT logic and API usage for consistency with current minimum Java version support.
summary: This commit modernizes internal JDT UI code by replacing hardcoded Java 1.1/1.3/1.4/1.5 version checks and validation constants with Java 1.8. This aligns internal logic with the ECJ compiler's minimum supported Java version. The transformation involves replacing boolean method calls like `JavaModelUtil.is1d4OrHigher()` with `true` (as they are always true for Java 8+) and replacing `JavaCore.VERSION_1_X` constants with `JavaCore.VERSION_1_8` in `JavaConventions.validate...` method arguments and variable initializers. These are direct 1:1 expression replacements, fully implementable as GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules.

commitMessage:
Handle < > brackets unconditionally

Remove checks whether running on Java 1.5+ to handle them as ECJ
mandates 1.8 thus this is always true.
Removed tests for pre 1.5 behavior and enable tests in
JavaPairMatcherTest that were not executed at all.
categoryReason: Refactoring internal JDT UI logic due to updated Java version baseline, involving removal of fields, methods, and conditional blocks.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires ability to remove fields/methods, or replace entire `if/else` blocks, or more sophisticated flow analysis to determine when conditional logic can be entirely removed due to always-true/false predicates. DSL currently only supports expression-level transformations.
summary: This commit simplifies internal JDT UI logic by removing conditional code paths related to Java 1.5+ feature checks (like angular bracket auto-closing/highlighting). Since JDT's minimum supported version is 1.8, these checks are obsolete. This involves deleting internal boolean fields (`fCloseAngularBrackets`, `fHighlightAngularBrackets`), their corresponding setter methods, and `if` conditions that relied on these fields or `JavaCore.compareJavaVersions()`. These are structural changes (field/method deletion, block removal) that are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for direct automation.

commitMessage:
Stop assuming Java 1.3 source if project doesn't set explicitly

ECJ supports 1.8 as min java version thus this would prevent the case of
UI generating/accepting code that the compiler may reject at compile
time.
categoryReason: Refactoring of internal JDT logic and API usage for consistency with current minimum Java version support.
summary: This commit consistently updates internal JDT UI code to standardize the default Java source/compliance version used in `JavaConventions` utility methods from Java 1.3 (and one 1.1) to Java 1.8. This ensures validation logic aligns with the ECJ compiler's minimum supported Java 1.8 requirement. The changes are direct 1:1 constant replacements within method arguments and variable initializers. This pattern is a duplicate of a significant part of the changes identified in Commit 0.

commitMessage:
Default to JAVA 8

Ecj supports min Java 8 so testing/supporting older versions in UI
creates the risk of generating/accepting code that compiler may reject.
categoryReason: Updating internal compliance defaults is an API configuration change.
summary: This commit updates internal JDT UI code to reflect a minimum Java 8 baseline. It replaces hardcoded Java 1.4 and 1.5 version constants with Java 1.8 in compliance checks and preference settings. Additionally, it removes a redundant version-check condition when adding imports, simplifying the boolean expression. These are direct constant and expression replacements, making them fully implementable as GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules. The rules are added to the existing 'eclipse-api-configuration.sandbox-hint' file and contribute to JDT internal API modernization.

commitMessage:
Stop support for pre 1.8 source in some editor features

This is only code level cleanup as it is impossible for anyone to use as
this was consulting project which can't not be pre 1.8 already.
categoryReason: Refactoring of internal JDT UI components to remove deprecated functionality.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires DSL support for removing interface implementations and entire method declarations, including conditional method calls.
summary: This commit removes support for pre-Java 8 source versions in several JDT editor features. It involves significant structural changes, including removing implementations of the `ISourceVersionDependent` interface from `JavaPairMatcher` and `JavaDoubleClickSelector`, removing the `setSourceVersion` method declarations, and eliminating code paths that relied on `fSelectAnnotations` for version-dependent behavior. These changes are structural modifications (interface removal, method deletion) that are beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL, classifying this commit as RED.

commitMessage:
Simplify JavaDoubleClickSelector

There is no need to shadow Character methods. Static import them and use
directly.
categoryReason: Refactoring of internal JDT UI components to simplify method calls and remove boilerplate.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires DSL support for removing entire method declarations.
summary: This commit simplifies `JavaDoubleClickSelector` by removing redundant private helper methods (`isIdentifierStart`, `isIdentifierPart`, `isWhitespace`) that merely shadowed `Character` class methods. The code is updated to use static imports of the `Character` methods directly. The core transformation involves removing these method declarations, which is not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL. While the method *call sites* could be updated, the architectural change of removing the methods themselves makes this a RED classification.

commitMessage:
Simplify JavaCodeScanner

Java 8 is the min version ECJ supports thus having complicated way to
add "assert"(1.4) and "enum"(1.5) is not needed and can be added to
default keywords.
categoryReason: Simplifying internal keyword handling in JDT's code scanner.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires DSL support for modifying array literal elements (insertion), removing field declarations, and removing blocks of statements.
summary: This commit simplifies `JavaCodeScanner` by directly including 'assert' and 'enum' in the default keyword list and removing the version-dependent logic that previously handled these keywords for Java 1.4 and 1.5. This involves modifying array literals, removing field declarations, and deleting blocks of statements. These are structural changes that are beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL, classifying this commit as RED.

commitMessage:
Always propose generics

Java 8 is the min version supported by ECJ thus generics are always
supported.
irrelevantReason: Internal architectural change - removal of conditional logic and a helper method based on updated Java baseline support (Java 8+) in JDT. The DSL cannot express the removal of entire `if` blocks or methods, nor does it capture architectural shifts in how the IDE's features (like generics proposal) are implemented based on its own minimum Java version support.
summary: The commit removes internal JDT UI logic that checked if generics proposals should be offered based on Java 5+ source versions. Since JDT's minimum supported version is now Java 8, this check became redundant and was removed. This is a structural refactoring of internal JDT code, not a pattern for user code transformation.

commitMessage: Reduce Java 1.5 helper checks in JavaModelUtils
categoryReason: The change is an internal refactoring within Eclipse JDT UI code, simplifying a boolean expression based on updated assumptions about the minimum Java version.
summary: The commit simplifies a boolean conditional expression within `NewJavaProjectWizardPageOne.java`. The original condition `!selectedCompliance.equals(jvmCompliance) && (JavaModelUtil.is50OrHigher(selectedCompliance) || JavaModelUtil.is50OrHigher(jvmCompliance))` is replaced with `!selectedCompliance.equals(jvmCompliance)`. This logical simplification is possible because `JavaModelUtil.is50OrHigher()` will always return `true` given the JDT's new Java 8 baseline. This is a direct 1:1 expression replacement, improving readability and minor performance, and is fully implementable as a TriggerPattern DSL rule.

commitMessage:
Remove dead code

Java 1.8 is mandated by ECJ thus removed code is non-reachable anymore.
categoryReason: The commit's primary goal is to remove code that is no longer reachable or relevant due to the mandated Java 8 baseline. This involves removing entire conditional blocks (`if` statements), removing boolean sub-expressions from `&&` or `||` conditions because they now evaluate to constant true/false, and removing unused internal utility methods/fields. These transformations require semantic analysis to determine which code is 'dead' or which boolean expressions are tautologies/contradictions, which is beyond the current TriggerPattern DSL's capabilities for direct pattern matching and rewriting. The DSL does not perform control flow analysis or dead code detection across statements.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires advanced static analysis capabilities within the DSL to detect and remove dead code branches, or a more sophisticated boolean algebra simplification engine that can rewrite larger conditional structures based on constant evaluation of sub-expressions. For example, replacing `if (ALWAYS_FALSE) { ... }` with nothing, or `if (ALWAYS_TRUE) { STATEMENT; }` with `STATEMENT;`.
summary: This commit removes various internal JDT checks for Java versions older than 1.8 (Java 8), such as `JavaModelUtil.is50OrHigher()` and `JavaModelUtil.isVersionLessThan(..., JavaCore.VERSION_1_5)`. Due to Java 8 being the new mandated baseline for ECJ, these conditions now always evaluate to `true` or `false` respectively, making the associated code unreachable or the conditions redundant. The changes involve removing entire `if` blocks, simplifying complex boolean expressions, and deleting unused internal helper methods and fields. This is fundamentally a dead code removal and logical simplification refactoring, which is not directly expressible in the current TriggerPattern DSL's pattern-matching capabilities, as it would require advanced control flow and semantic analysis.

commitMessage:
Remove pre 1.8 support

ECJ supports min 1.8 and JDT UI provides no way to set BREE lower than
that thus all this code is non reachable.
categoryReason: This commit, like Commit 0, focuses on removing code that has become dead or redundant due to the updated Java 8 baseline. This involves complex logical simplifications in `if` conditions (e.g., removing `|| false` or `&& true` clauses) and removing entire `if` blocks whose conditions become constant. The DSL's current capabilities are limited to syntactic pattern matching and replacement of expressions, annotations, or method declarations, not semantic analysis for dead code or complex control-flow simplification.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires advanced static analysis within the DSL to identify and remove unreachable code paths or simplify boolean expressions based on compile-time constant evaluation, effectively pruning dead conditional branches. This would require an understanding of control flow beyond simple AST node matching.
summary: This commit removes code related to pre-Java 8 support, specifically targeting internal checks like `JavaModelUtil.is1d6OrHigher()` and `JavaCore.compareJavaVersions(JavaCore.VERSION_1_5, ...)`. With Java 8 as the minimum required version, these checks become redundant, leading to the removal of conditional logic, `if` statements, and associated helper methods and fields. This is a dead code elimination and logical simplification effort within internal JDT UI components. The nature of these changes, which involve complex conditional logic and structural removals, is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for direct rule-based automation.

commitMessage:
Simplify "string" generation/cleanups by dropping pre 1.5

ECJ mandates 1.8+ and JDT UI converts compiler settings to that
unconditionally so this code is simply non-reachable.
irrelevantReason: Internal dead code removal due to Java 8+ baseline. The changes involve removing conditional logic and assignments that are no longer reachable, which is a structural refactoring of the plugin's own code rather than a reusable transformation pattern for user code.
summary: This commit removes internal Java 5 compatibility checks (e.g., `JavaModelUtil.is50OrHigher`) and related conditional logic within JDT UI's `StringBuilderCleanUp` and `GenerateToStringAction`. These code paths are considered dead because the Eclipse Compiler for Java (ECJ) now mandates Java 8 or higher. This is a structural cleanup of the plugin's own codebase, not a generalizable code transformation pattern for user code. Therefore, it is classified as not applicable for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:
Remove pre1.6 support from string generation

ECJ no longer supports pre 1.8 and JDT UI simply converts project
settings to 1.8 if lower thus all this code is non reachable.
irrelevantReason: Internal dead code removal due to Java 8+ baseline. The changes involve removing conditional logic and assignments that are no longer reachable, which is a structural refactoring of the plugin's own code rather than a reusable transformation pattern for user code.
summary: This commit removes internal Java 6 compatibility checks (e.g., `JavaModelUtil.isVersionLessThan(version, JavaCore.VERSION_1_6)`) and related code in `GenerateToStringAction.java`. Similar to the previous commit, this is a cleanup of dead code within the JDT UI plugin itself, driven by the Java 8+ baseline mandated by ECJ. It does not introduce a reusable code transformation pattern for user code and is thus not applicable for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:
Remove last Java 5 checks from UI

Having code to show errors if Java 5 JVM when Java UI migrates projects
to Java 8 is pointless.
categoryReason: The rules simplify internal JDT UI code by removing redundant Java version checks that are always true given the Java 8+ baseline.
summary: This commit removes various internal Java 5 compatibility checks from JDT UI, specifically targeting `JavaModelUtil.is50OrHigher` related logic and corresponding NLS messages. The core transformation is the simplification of boolean expressions involving `org.eclipse.jdt.internal.corext.util.JavaModelUtil.is1d7OrHigher($project)`. Since ECJ now mandates a Java 8+ baseline, this method effectively always evaluates to true. The rules capture this by replacing `JavaModelUtil.is1d7OrHigher($project)` with `true` and its negation `!JavaModelUtil.is1d7OrHigher($project)` with `false`. These are direct, semantically equivalent expression replacements for internal JDT code, improving clarity and removing dead code paths. The remaining changes (removing NLS keys, structural `if-else` blocks) are not suitable for DSL rules.

commitMessage:
Remove Java 7 checks

ECJ support 1.8 as lowest version and JDT UI converts projects to that
as a min level thus this code is non reachable.
categoryReason: The rules simplify internal JDT UI code by removing redundant Java version checks that are always true given the Java 8+ baseline.
summary: This commit extensively removes Java 7 compatibility checks (`org.eclipse.jdt.internal.corext.util.JavaModelUtil.is1d7OrHigher`) from various JDT UI components, including quick assists, cleanups (`HashCleanUp`, `ObjectsEqualsCleanUp`, `TryWithResourceCleanUp`), and refactoring actions (`SurroundWithTryMultiCatchAction`). Similar to the previous commit, this is a large-scale internal refactoring based on the Java 8+ baseline of ECJ. The core transformation is the simplification of boolean expressions where `JavaModelUtil.is1d7OrHigher($project)` effectively becomes `true` (and its negation becomes `false`). These are direct, semantically equivalent expression replacements for internal JDT code, covered by the same DSL rules as proposed in Commit 2. Therefore, this commit is classified as a duplicate of Commit 2 for its DSL-relevant patterns.

commitMessage:
Remove usages of JavaModelUtil.is1d8OrHigher from
o.e.jdt.ui

ECJ mandates 1.8 thus this code is not reachable.
categoryReason: The change involves modifying internal Eclipse JDT UI code to remove redundant checks based on the baseline Java version, rather than introducing new user-facing features or API migrations.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support implicit logical simplification (e.g., `true && X => X`, `X || false => X`) and `if (false)` block removal to cover all transformations. The current rules directly target the combined effect of these simplifications where possible. For instance, the rule `org.eclipse.jdt.internal.corext.util.JavaModelUtil.is1d8OrHigher($proj) => true` helps simplify, but expressions like `X || false` would require an additional logical simplification rule.
summary: This commit removes redundant calls to `JavaModelUtil.is1d8OrHigher()` because the ECJ compiler mandates Java 8, making the check always true. The patterns include simplifying ternary operators, removing negated conditions from boolean `||` expressions, removing positive conditions from boolean `&&` expressions, and directly replacing the method call with `true`. While direct removal of entire `if (false)` blocks and full variable propagation are not directly expressible in the DSL, a significant portion of the logical simplifications can be achieved with these expression-level rules. The rules assume `JavaModelUtil.is1d8OrHigher($proj)` always evaluates to `true`.

commitMessage:
More removals of JavaModelUtil.is1d8OrHigher

Ecj mandates 1.8 thus such guarded code is not reachable
categoryReason: The commit modifies an internal JDT UI method call by removing a parameter, reflecting internal API evolution.
summary: This commit removes the `ICompilationUnit` argument (`$cu`) from calls to the `SplitTryResourceFixCore.initialConditionsCheck()` method. This is an internal JDT API modernization, likely because the `initialConditionsCheck` method's internal logic for Java 8+ version checks (which previously relied on the `ICompilationUnit`) is now redundant due to the ECJ baseline. This is a direct 1:1 method call argument removal, fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. The rule assumes the target method's signature has been updated to remove the first parameter.

commitMessage:
Final bits of pre 1.8 support removal in JavaModelUtils

Removing non reachable code.
categoryReason: The changes simplify internal JDT UI logic related to compiler compliance by removing a redundant version check, which aligns with internal API modernization and code cleanup.
summary: This commit updates an internal JDT UI class (`ComplianceConfigurationBlock`) to remove pre-Java 1.8 support. Specifically, it replaces a local variable declaration `boolean enabled = JavaModelUtil.is1d8OrHigher(target);` with `boolean enabled = true;`. This simplifies internal logic by eliminating a redundant version check, as the code now assumes Java 8 or higher. The pattern is a direct 1:1 replacement of a local variable declaration's initializer. Other changes in the commit involve complex structural modifications to `if` blocks, which are beyond the current DSL's capabilities (RED).

commitMessage:
Move away of deprecated Locale constructor

Use Locale.of as preferred way instead to reduce new warnings after move
to Java 21
categoryReason: The change replaces a deprecated Java API constructor with its modern factory method equivalent, specifically introduced in Java 19.
summary: This commit replaces deprecated `new java.util.Locale(...)` constructors with the recommended `java.util.Locale.of(...)` static factory method. This is a direct 1:1 API replacement, semantically safe, and improves code readability and adherence to modern API usage introduced in Java 19. The patterns cover 1, 2, and 3-argument constructors, including cases with empty string arguments. This transformation is fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL and is a duplicate of existing rules in `java19-deprecations.sandbox-hint`.

commitMessage:
Fix merge of if conditions to guard against pattern instanceof (#1931)

- fix MergeConditionalBlocksCleanup to handle instanceof patterns and
  not merge them when they have same name
- add new test to CleanUpTest16
- fixes #1200
categoryReason: This commit fixes a bug in an existing JDT cleanup by modifying its internal logic. It does not introduce a new transformation pattern for user code.
languageChangeNeeded: This change is a bug fix and structural refactoring within an existing cleanup's Java implementation. It involves extracting an anonymous inner class, adding new inner classes, modifying method signatures, and implementing complex conditional logic to prevent incorrect refactorings. These types of changes cannot be expressed using the current pattern-matching DSL.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the 'Merge Conditional Blocks' cleanup. It refactors the cleanup's internal logic to prevent incorrect merging of 'if' statements that use Java 16+ 'instanceof' patterns with pattern variables, which could introduce naming conflicts. The changes involve extracting an anonymous ASTVisitor to a named inner class, adding new logic for tracking pattern variable names, and modifying internal conditional checks within the cleanup's implementation. This is a bug fix and structural refactoring of the cleanup itself, not a transformation pattern for user code, hence it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Asynchronously decorate quick outline view via DecorationManager #1922

The Quick Outline View currently applies (i.e., calculates and draws)
decorations for override indicators synchronously. In case the
calculation of the indicator takes long, it blocks the UI.
The ordinary Outline View defers the responsibility of calculating and
applying decorations to the DecorationManager. This ensures that (1)
that task is performed asynchronously via a dedicated job and (2) that
the decoration manager takes care of the current user configuration
(i.e., whether those indicators shall be shown or not), which the Quick
Outline View currently replicates.

With this change, the Quick Outline View uses the same label decorator
as the ordinary Outline View, which makes the DecorationManager
process the override label decorator asynchronously instead of applying
it synchronously.

Contributes to https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/1922
summary: This commit modernizes the Quick Outline View's label decoration by replacing `ColoringLabelProvider` with `DecoratingJavaLabelProvider`. This change offloads decoration calculation and application to Eclipse's `DecorationManager`, ensuring asynchronous processing and respecting user configuration, thereby preventing UI blocks. The transformation is a direct constructor call replacement, enhancing performance and leveraging modern Eclipse UI architecture, and is fully expressible as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. The DSL automatically handles import changes.

commitMessage:
fix executor resource warning since 19

Potential resource leak: 'executor' may not be closed at this location
languageChangeNeeded: DSL needs richer support for structural transformations of try-catch-finally blocks to try-with-resources, including moving declarations and transforming `finally` block content (e.g., handling `shutdownNow()` calls for `AutoCloseable` resources like `ExecutorService`).
summary: This commit refactors resource management by converting a manual `ExecutorService` shutdown in a `finally` block into a `try-with-resources` statement. This improves reliability and reduces boilerplate for `AutoCloseable` resources (ExecutorService implements `AutoCloseable` since Java 19). The transformation involves complex structural changes to the AST, including moving variable declarations into the `try` header and removing the `finally` block, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL's pattern-based replacements. Thus, it's classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Fix duplicate add suppress warnings proposals (#1942)

- add new abstract method alreadyHasProposal() to
  SuppressWarningsBaseSubProcessor to check if a proposal has already
  been added
- add implemention of alreadyHasProposal() to
  SuppressWarningsSubProcessor
- add new test to QuickFixTest1d8
- fixes #1935
categoryReason: The change directly modifies an internal JDT boolean expression to fix a bug in quick-fix proposal generation logic.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in JDT's quick-fix proposal logic by extending a boolean condition to correctly detect deprecated constructor usage alongside deprecated method usage, preventing duplicate "add @SuppressWarnings" proposals. The pattern is a direct 1:1 expression replacement of a boolean condition, fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. Other changes involve introducing new classes and abstract methods which are structural and not suitable for DSL rules.

commitMessage:
Deprecate for removal JavaPlugin.getJavaCorePluginPreferences

Class org.eclipse.core.runtime.Preferences has been deprecated for years
and method getJavaCorePluginPreferencesNew (returning
IEclipsePreferences) is there.
Migrated unit tests to use getJavaCorePluginPreferencesNew.
categoryReason: This is a direct API modernization pattern for an internal Eclipse JDT API.
summary: This commit deprecates the `JavaPlugin.getJavaCorePluginPreferences()` method by adding an `@Deprecated` annotation and updating Javadoc. The commit message indicates that unit tests were migrated to use `getJavaCorePluginPreferencesNew()`, which is the replacement API. While the provided diff only shows the API definition change, the implied code transformation for migrating call sites is a direct 1:1 method call replacement, which is fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. This exact transformation pattern has been previously identified and exists as a DSL rule in `eclipse-api-modernization.sandbox-hint`, making this a duplicate.

commitMessage:
Migrate ContentAssistHistory to IEclipsePreferences

Long deprecated o.e.core.runtime.Preferences class would better not be
used anymore.
categoryReason: Migration from deprecated Eclipse API to modern alternatives.
summary: This commit migrates usage of the deprecated `org.eclipse.core.runtime.Preferences` API to `org.eclipse.core.runtime.preferences.IEclipsePreferences`. The core transformations involve replacing `JavaPlugin.getPluginPreferences()` with `InstanceScope.INSTANCE.getNode(JavaPlugin.getPluginId())`, converting `preferences.setValue(key, value)` to `((IEclipsePreferences)preferences).put(key, value)`, and changing `preferences.getString(key)` to `((IEclipsePreferences)preferences).get(key, "")`. These are direct 1:1 API replacements, improving adherence to modern Eclipse API practices and handling deprecation. The transformation is fully implementable as three GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules, using `inClass` and `instanceof` guards for precision. This could partially replace functionalities related to deprecated Eclipse API usage in a plugin like `sandbox_platform_helper` by using DSL rules for simple API migration. The `sourceVersion` is 8 as `IEclipsePreferences` is not a new Java language feature.

commitMessage:
Do not expand same class twice in (quick) type hierarchy

Fixes eclipse-jdt#1830
categoryReason: Fixes an internal bug within a specific JDT UI component.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support arbitrary statement insertion/wrapping within a lambda expression, including calls to newly introduced methods. It also needs to support the definition of new fields and methods within a class (or anonymous class) for more complex refactorings.
summary: This commit fixes a bug where the same class could be expanded twice in the JDT type hierarchy view. It introduces a `visited` set to prevent redundant expansions. The primary code changes involve wrapping `updateContent()` calls with `preUpdateContent()` and `postUpdateContent()` within a `JavaCore.runReadOnly()` lambda, and adding new helper methods (`shouldExpand`, `preUpdateContent`, `postUpdateContent`) and a `visited` field to `TypeHierarchyViewer` (or its anonymous subclass). These structural changes, particularly inserting multiple statements within a lambda body and defining new class members, are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, this is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
WiP this commit needs to be squashed.

- Wrap execution in new private method
TypeHierarchyViewer.runUsingVisited(Runnable)
- Make TypeHierarchyViewer.updateContent package-protected and final
- Let the subclasses hook into it by implementing hookUpdateContent
instead
categoryReason: Internal architectural changes and API restructuring within JDT.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support method renaming, changing method modifiers in a hierarchy, introducing new methods, and more complex restructuring of lambda bodies involving multiple statements and wrapping.
summary: This commit represents a significant internal refactoring of the JDT Type Hierarchy viewer. It reworks the `updateContent` method hierarchy, introduces a new `runUsingVisited` helper method to manage a `visited` set, and simplifies the lambda in `TypeHierarchyViewPart` by reverting to a single `updateContent` call. The refactoring involves method renaming across a class hierarchy, changing method modifiers, introducing new private methods, and complex restructuring of lambda bodies. These changes are structural and architectural, going beyond simple expression or statement pattern replacements. The DSL currently lacks the capabilities to perform such deep refactorings (e.g., renaming methods, changing modifiers, adding new methods, or handling complex multi-statement lambda body transformations). Therefore, this commit is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Make JavaPlugin.getActivePage() aware of not running platform and inline

Currently JavaPlugin.getActivePage() fails if no workbench is running,
while the method itself can return null if there is no active window
anyways. Beside that is unnecessary access the default java plugin even
though all code called there is static anyways.

This now changes the following parts:

1) guard getActiveWorkbenchWindow against not running workbench
2) used getActiveWorkbenchWindow instead of similar code in
getActivePage like in getActiveWorkbenchShell
3) delete no longer needed private method
4) Use JavaPlugin.getActiveWorkbenchWindow() instead of PlatformUI
5) Use JavaPlugin.getActiveWorkbenchShell() instead of PlatformUI
categoryReason: Modernizing usage of Eclipse Platform UI API by routing through internal JavaPlugin helper methods.
summary: This commit modernizes Eclipse JDT UI code by replacing direct calls to `PlatformUI.getWorkbench().getActiveWorkbenchWindow().getActivePage()`, `.getShell()`, and `.getActiveWorkbenchWindow()` with their corresponding static wrapper methods in `org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.JavaPlugin`. This centralizes UI access, potentially making it more robust against a non-running workbench. The transformations are direct 1:1 method call chain replacements, which are fully expressible as GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules. The internal changes within `JavaPlugin.java` itself (inlining a private method, adding `if` guards) are structural and not directly expressible by the DSL.

commitMessage:
BestMatchHover: add code snippet to error msg

for https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/issues/1163
categoryReason: The change introduces robust error handling by wrapping existing code in a try-catch block to prevent UI freezes and log exceptions.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs support for wrapping arbitrary statement blocks with new control flow constructs (e.g., try-catch statements, if-else blocks), including the insertion of new statements into the catch/else block. Current DSL only supports expression-level replacements and simple multi-line continuations, not arbitrary statement insertions or structural wraps.
summary: This commit wraps a complex multi-statement block within a `try-catch (RuntimeException)` block to add robust error handling and logging using `SelectionUtil.logException()`. This structural transformation, which involves inserting new control flow statements and a new statement block, is explicitly not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL's capabilities for pattern-based replacements.

commitMessage:
JavaIndenter: fix BadLocationException in first line - fixes 1955

Used to happen in breakpoint condition

https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/1955
categoryReason: The change directly addresses a `BadLocationException` by adding a boundary check.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would require extensions to introduce new `if` control flow statements and their associated blocks, as well as new local variable declarations as part of the replacement pattern. Currently, the DSL primarily supports expression-level replacements and simple structural changes.
summary: This commit fixes a `BadLocationException` in `JavaIndenter.java` by adding an `if (lineOfOffset != 0)` condition before accessing `fDocument.getLineOffset(lineOfOffset - 1)`. This prevents an out-of-bounds access on the first line of the document. The change involves introducing new local variables and conditional control flow, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based transformations. Hence, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
fix NPE in RenameInformationPopup with eclipse restart #1897

https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/1897
categoryReason: The change directly addresses `NullPointerException`s by adding null checks.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would require extensions to introduce new local variable declarations (e.g., `IWorkbenchWindow workbenchWindow = ...`) and conditional control flow (`if (variable == null) { return; }`) as part of the replacement pattern. Currently, the DSL is limited to expression-level replacements or simple structural changes.
summary: This commit fixes NullPointerExceptions in `RenameInformationPopup.java` and `RenameLinkedMode.java` that could occur during Eclipse restarts. It adds `null` checks for `fEditor.getSite().getWorkbenchWindow()` and `fEditor.getViewer()` respectively, preventing method calls on potentially `null` objects. This involves introducing new local variables and `if` statements, which the current TriggerPattern DSL does not support for pattern-based transformations. Hence, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Adjust to JavaCore.defaultRootModules deprecation

Use the new method that filters based on the release. If release option
is not set, use target and if it's not there too use the latest version.
categoryReason: The change involves updating a deprecated Eclipse JDT API (`JavaCore.defaultRootModules`) to its modern version by adding a new argument.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need significant extensions to dynamically generate complex, multi-line conditional logic for a method argument (e.g., `String release = ... if (release == null) ...`). While the simple act of adding an argument to a method call is supported, generating the argument's value through such conditional logic is not. The DSL also doesn't support automatic generation of local variable declarations from complex expressions.
summary: This commit updates internal JDT UI code to address the deprecation of `JavaCore.defaultRootModules()`. It replaces calls to the 1-argument version with a new 2-argument version, dynamically determining the `release` string argument based on project options (`COMPILER_RELEASE`, `COMPILER_CODEGEN_TARGET_PLATFORM`) or `JavaCore.latestSupportedJavaVersion()`. This dynamic argument generation involves multiple local variable assignments and `if` statements, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based transformations. Additionally, a Java 16+ pattern matching `instanceof` (`IPackageFragmentRoot el`) is introduced, but the primary challenge is the argument generation. Hence, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Add new feature to convert pattern instanceof to switch (#1972)

* Add new feature to convert pattern instanceof to switch

- add new PatternInstanceofToSwitchCleanUpCore and
  PatternInstanceofToSwitchFixCore classes to convert
  a nesting if statement using pattern instanceof expressions
  into either a switch statement or switch expression if possible
- add new cleanup to JavaFeatureTabPage, plugin, and CleanUpConstants
- add rtstubs_21.jar for testing
- add new CleanUpTest21 class and add tests for new feature
- fixes #1919
categoryReason: The change introduces a new modern Java syntax feature and associated cleanup.
dslRule: Cannot be fully expressed as a simple pattern replacement. The core transformation is converting a multi-statement `if-else if` chain of `instanceof` patterns into a `switch` statement or expression. This involves complex AST structural manipulation, including matching multiple statements, extracting variables, creating new control flow constructs, and moving entire code blocks, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need significant extensions to support matching and rewriting multi-statement control flow blocks (e.g., IfStatement to SwitchStatement/SwitchExpression). This would involve capabilities to analyze and manipulate sequences of statements, extract variables into new contexts (e.g., switch 'case' patterns), and construct complex control flow structures.
summary: This commit adds a new JDT cleanup feature to convert `instanceof` pattern matching in `if-else if` chains into `switch` statements or expressions (Java 21+). The diff primarily shows the addition of NLS keys and UI elements to enable this cleanup, not the transformation logic itself. The underlying transformation involves complex structural changes to control flow, including matching multiple conditional statements, extracting bound variables, and creating new switch blocks with corresponding case labels. This is beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on expression-level and simple statement-level replacements. Therefore, this is classified as RED. The `sourceVersion` is 21 due to the specific Java version targeted by this new feature as indicated by `rtstubs_21.jar`.

languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need advanced capabilities to match and rewrite complex multi-statement blocks, including conditional logic and variable flow tracking, into a single, potentially complex, expression. This is a major extension not currently supported.
summary: This commit fixes an internal bug related to determining the `release` option in JDT's module dialogs. It refactors a multi-line `if-else if` cascade for deriving the Java release version into a single, more concise ternary expression. While improving code readability and correctness, this transformation involves replacing a sequence of several statements and their control flow with a single complex expression. This level of structural rewrite is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on expression-level and simple statement-level replacements. Therefore, this is classified as RED. The code deals with Java modules, introduced in Java 9, which is the implicit source context.

commitMessage:
SemanticHighlightingPresenter: prevent BadLocationException

when document was changed when async execution happens.
Happens sometimes during SaveParticipantTest
categoryReason: The change prevents a specific exception by adding a robust check.
dslRule:
The change involves three distinct parts:
1.  Introducing a new local variable: `String contents = document.get();`.
2.  Modifying an existing boolean `if` condition: `fSourceViewer != null && document == fSourceViewer.getDocument()` is extended to `fSourceViewer != null && document == fSourceViewer.getDocument() && Objects.equals(contents, document.get())`.
3.  Replacing a simple `runnable.run();` statement with a complex `if-else` block that includes `instanceof` with pattern variables and a `synchronized` block.

The TriggerPattern DSL does not support inserting new local variable declarations (part 1) or matching and rewriting complex control flow structures (part 3). While part 2 (modifying the boolean condition) *could* be expressed if the `contents` variable already existed in the matched scope, it does not. The `dslRule` field cannot define a new variable and use it within the replacement expression if that variable is not bound by the pattern. Therefore, the transformation cannot be fully implemented in the current DSL.
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL needs capabilities for:
1.  Introducing new local variable declarations as part of a transformation.
2.  Matching and rewriting complex `IfStatement` blocks, including nested `if`, `instanceof` with pattern variables (JLS16+), and `synchronized` blocks. This would require more advanced AST manipulation features than currently available for simple pattern replacement.
summary: This commit fixes a `BadLocationException` in `SemanticHighlightingReconciler.java` by making an asynchronous UI update more robust against document changes. This involves: 1) Adding a new local variable `String contents = document.get();` before the `asyncExec` lambda. 2) Extending the `if` condition within the lambda to include `&& Objects.equals(contents, document.get())`. 3) Wrapping the `runnable.run()` call in a new, complex `if-else` block that includes `instanceof` pattern matching and `synchronized` blocks for thread safety. While the modification of the boolean `if` condition (part 2) is a simple expression change, it depends on the newly introduced `contents` variable (part 1), which cannot be created by the DSL. Parts 1 and 3 involve injecting new statements and complex control flow, which are beyond the current DSL's capabilities. Thus, this commit is classified as RED. The `sourceVersion` is 8 as it's an internal JDT fix, not dependent on new external Java language features beyond the baseline.
dslValidationResult: Parse error at line 1: Rule starting at line 1 is missing ';;' terminator (line 1)

commitMessage:
Add new quick assist for pattern instanceof if chain to switch (#1980)

- modify PatternInstanceofToSwitchFixCore to add new static
  method createPatternInstanceofToSwitchFix()
- add new code to QuickAssistProcessor that looks to see if the
  cursor is in an if chain and see if it is using pattern instanceof
  and can be converted to switch
- add new test AssistQuickFixTest21
- fixes #1978
categoryReason: Introduces a new quick assist for a complex structural transformation leveraging Java 16+ pattern instanceof to convert if-chains to switch statements/expressions.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires support for matching and transforming multi-statement control flow blocks (if-else chains) into other complex control flow blocks (switch statements/expressions), including advanced variable binding and introduction of new local variables from pattern matches.
summary: This commit adds a new quick assist to convert an `if-chain` using `instanceof` patterns into a `switch` statement or expression. This is a complex structural transformation that involves matching multiple `IfStatement` nodes, analyzing `instanceof` conditions (including pattern variables for Java 16+), and generating a new `SwitchStatement`/`Expression` with `case` labels. This level of complex control flow transformation, particularly involving arbitrary length if-chains and creating entirely new structured statements, is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
fix IndexOutOfBoundsException in OrganizeImportsAction.doChooseImports

When skipping an import except the last.

fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/1996
categoryReason: Optimizes collection API usage.
summary: This commit fixes an `IndexOutOfBoundsException` in `OrganizeImportsAction.doChooseImports` and includes several code modernizations. The most complex parts involve refactoring `Display.syncExec` to `Display.syncCall` with subsequent chaining and adopting Java 16's pattern matching for `instanceof`. These are deemed RED as they require complex structural rewrites or DSL extensions not currently available. However, a clearly extractable and reusable pattern is the modernization of `Collection.toArray()` usage. The pattern involves replacing `collection.toArray(new Type[0])` with `collection.toArray(Type[]::new)`. This is a standard Java 11+ idiom that improves readability and can offer minor performance benefits. This is a direct 1:1 expression replacement, fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL, and complements existing rules in `collection-toarray.sandbox-hint`.
